data.title: AZZ Inc. in Texas introduces new stock repurchase program in 2026. Board approved initiative to buy back $100 million shares.
data.description: In Fort Worth, Texas on January 30, 2026, AZZ Inc. (NYSE: AZZ), a top provider of hot-dip galvanizing and coil coating services, revealed the approval by its Board of Directors of a fresh stock repurchase initiative, known as the "2026 Share Repurchase Program."

data.title: New Gold receives final court approval for arrangement with Coeur Mining; approved by Supreme Court of British Columbia.
data.description: In Toronto on January 30, 2026, New Gold Inc. (TSX: NGD) (NYSE: NGD) shared that the Supreme Court of British Columbia finalized approval for the Company's plan of arrangement under the Business Corporations Act.

data.title: Origin Agritech reports fiscal year 2025 results, files Form 20-F. (NASDAQ: SEED). Year ended Sep 30.
data.description: Origin Agritech Ltd., a prominent Chinese agricultural technology company, disclosed its financial performance for the year concluding on September 30. The company, trade identified as SEED on NASDAQ, submitted its Annual Report on Form 20-F for the fiscal year 2025. The filing was made on January 30, 2026, providing stakeholders and investors with comprehensive insights into the company's operations and results. This marks a significant step towards transparency, accountability, and regulatory compliance for Origin Agritech Ltd.

data.title: NexPoint Residential Trust (NYSE: NXRT) discloses 2025 dividend income tax treatment on common stock.
data.description: Today, NexPoint Residential Trust, Inc. (NYSE: NXRT) disclosed the definitive income allocations for its 2025 dividend distributions on common stock. This information is crucial for shareholders to accurately report their earnings. This announcement comes after the company's successful dividend distributions in 2025, providing transparency and clarity to investors.

data.title: BankFirst Capital Corporation reports $10.17M earnings in Q4 and $28.68M for 2025. Net income per common share was $1.74.
data.description: Headquartered in Columbus, Mississippi, a financial institution named BankFirst Capital Corporation (OTCQX: BFCC) recently disclosed its financial performance. The parent company of BankFirst Financial Services in Macon reported a net income of $10.17 million. This equated to earnings of $1.74 per common share during Q4 of 2026.

data.title: Gold crashes 12% due to $1.68B crypto liquidations. Market forces triggered by margin calls and trading algorithms. Structural bull case for precious metals remains strong. Bitcoin fall led to forced selling, impacting gold and silver markets. Precious metals still have strong fundamentals.
data.description: A recent incident, involving a 12% drop in gold and 33% drop in silver in one trading session, was caused by various factors aside from monetary policy. A significant portion of the drop was linked to a $1.68 billion wave of cryptocurrency margin calls, with 93% of trades being long positions being forced closed. This chain reaction was exacerbated by regulators raising margin requirements globally and trading algorithms creating a feedback loop. The crash started in Bitcoin futures markets on January 29, resulting in forced selling across exchanges. Portfolio margin accounts, used by hedge funds and pro traders to treat all bets as collateral, played a crucial role in linking the crypto crash to the precious metals selloff. This complex chain reaction underscores the importance of understanding market dynamics and the structural bull case for precious metals remains strong despite the leverage breakdown.

FED’S BOWMAN: RATE HOLD WAS A CLOSE CALL Fed Governor Michelle Bowman said holding rates steady was not an easy decision and reflected a need for more data. She said policy remains moderately restrictive, but after 75 basis points of cuts last year, the Fed can afford to wait.

data.title: Fundamental Advisors creates MorningSky Power to manage a 20 GW portfolio of solar and battery storage projects.
data.description: A newly created platform has been launched with the aim of progressing, enhancing, and managing a 20-gigawatt (GW) collection of large-scale solar and battery storage projects. The announcement was made in New York on January 30, 2026. Fundamental Advisors ("Fundamental"), a well-known alternative asset manager concentrating on municipal, public purpose, disclosed this establishment.

data.title: White Pearl Acquisition Corp. priced an initial public offering of $100 million, formed for mergers or acquisitions.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, White Pearl Acquisition Corp announced its formation as a blank check company registered in the British Virgin Islands. The company was created to facilitate a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, or recapitalization. The specific numbers or financial details were not disclosed in the provided information.

data.description: Farmland Partners Inc. (NYSE: FPI) in Denver disclosed the tax treatment of its 2025 common stock distributions. The company's CUSIP is 31154R109 and EIN is 46-3769850. For the 2025 Dividend Treatment of Common Stock, the details are as follows: Declaration Date, Payment Date, and Record Date. The Distribution Per Share for Taxable Ordinary and Qualified categories are outlined. The breakdown includes Capital Gains, Allocation to 2025, and Total values.

data.title: Vrtly, Inc. launches marketing platform for healthcare brands, aiming to eliminate "blind spots." Exciting news for medical aesthetics industry.
data.description: Today in Danville, California, on January 30, 2026, Vrtly, Inc. unveiled a new digital ecosystem aimed at providing healthcare brands with enhanced insight into the patient journey. The platform is the leading point-of-care marketing tool for medical aesthetics. This launch is a significant move for Vrtly, aiming to empower healthcare brands with unique visibility features.

data.title: Tune Joy, a puzzle video game merging music discovery, to launch in Spring in the US by Sonic Habitat.
data.description: In Miami on January 30, 2026, Sonic Habitat unveiled their latest mobile game, Tune Joy, in the United States. The game caters to a wide audience, including casual players and puzzle lovers. Tune Joy offers a unique experience with its user-friendly gameplay and detailed audiovisual elements.

data.title: Clarins hosts 3rd Galentine's party in LA to celebrate Lip Perfector, a peptide-infused gloss with vanilla scent in 16 shades.
data.description: In Los Angeles on January 30, 2026, the renowned French beauty brand Clarins organized its third yearly Galantines party. The event, themed Sugar & Shine, commemorated the brand's Lip Perfector – a gloss infused with peptides, boasting a delightful vanilla cupcake aroma. This product is offered in a range of 16 different hues.

data.title: Trump nominates Kevin Warsh as Fed chair to succeed Jerome Powell. The choice is key for the economy. -Colby Smith
data.description: Kevin Warsh has been nominated by President Trump as the successor to Jerome H. Powell as chair of the Federal Reserve. This decision holds significant importance for the economy, with Warsh set to potentially lead the central bank. Our reporter Colby Smith analyzes the implications of this nomination in the light of its potential impact on economic policies and decisions.

data.title: SoFi reports $1B record revenue in Q4 after reentering crypto market; introduced stablecoin, blockchain remittances.
data.description: The financial technology bank announced that it achieved a milestone with a quarterly revenue of $1 billion, marking a new record. This achievement coincided with the relaunch of cryptocurrency trading services, the introduction of a stablecoin to its platform, and the implementation of blockchain technology for remittance services.
US House Speaker Johnson seeks to hold votes on Senate deal Monday - Fox News

data.title: Rithm Acquisition Corp. got noncompliance notice from NYSE for having less than 300 public stockholders. 45-day grace period.
data.description: In New York, the Rithm Acquisition Corp. has reported that it was notified on January 26, 2026, by the New York Stock Exchange about non-compliance with Section 802.01B of the NYSE Listed Company Manual. This section mandates a consistent minimum of 300 public stockholders, which the company failed to maintain. As per NYSE protocols, the company has 45 days from receiving the notice to address this issue.

data.title: K2 Capital Acquisition Corporation closes upsized $138M IPO with 13.8M units at $10 each, including 1.8M additional units. KTWOU trades on Nasdaq with KTWOR and KTWO symbols.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, K2 Capital Acquisition Corporation completed an upsized initial public offering, selling 13,800,000 units at $10.00 each, raising a total of $138,000,000. This total includes the underwriters' full exercise of their option to purchase an additional 1,800,000 units. Each unit comprises one Class A ordinary share and one right to receive one-fifth of a Class A ordinary share upon the closing of the company’s initial business combination. Trading of the units began on January 29, 2026, on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol "KTWOU," while the Class A ordinary shares and rights are listed under the symbols "KTWO" and "KTWOR," respectively.
